name: Razvan-Adrian C.
education: Engineer's degree (pre-Bologna system), Computer Science from University POLITEHNICA of Bucharest
headline: Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft
about:
As a systems-level software engineer, I specialize in software design, leveraging C11 and C++17 to enhance performance, security, and system integrations. My passion lies in the holistic understanding and involvement in the systems I deliver, ideally positioning myself at the platform level. This role allows me to blend my knowledge of user-space applications with a solid understanding of various Linux kernel aspects, although I continuously seek to deepen this expertise.

I thrive on challenges that require a deep integration of system components and have a proven track record of delivering robust, high-quality software solutions. My goal is to contribute to projects where I can apply my diverse skill set to innovate and solve complex problems at the platform level, ensuring seamless, secure, and efficient system operation. Software development brings me deep satisfaction as opposed to other types of work that are adjacent.

I've done some recent incursions into Rust during my free time, which underscores my commitment to embracing modern technologies and methodologies that push the boundaries of performance and reliability. More recently I've been looking into integrating the Linux aspects I love from the Windows-based perspective that's required for my job. I still remain dedicated and proficient in Linux and use windows as a tool for my hobbies and media.

I sometimes write random stuff at: https://github.com/synapse-overload that helps me in my at-home study sessions and mini-projects, which I do when I'm not depleted after work or when I am but want to do things that are more interesting for me but out of scope for the job.

experiences.title: Senior Linux Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Linux Performance Software Engineer
Responsibilities:
- user perf events and monitoring with flame graphs to discover hot points for the applications we run in the cloud
- use FTrace to discover behaviours under certain loads or specific incoming network traffic when using specific XDP programs that are meant to bypass the linux network stack
- write C++ code for new features that increase performance of the network applications and refactor when necessary
- use DPDK library for integrating poll mode drivers for maximum capture speed when available
- prototype basic C applications for POCs
- use knowledge of packaging systems to migrate to newer kernels to discover whether newer kernel stacks offer higher speeds, maintain spec files for rpms
- add and integrate slight modifications to drivers to accomodate our needs
- make formal or informal presentations for technologies used for the whole team so that knowledge is spread out uniformly accross the team
- create wiki pages to document long investigations into legacy code and possible performance gains of portotype implementations
- keep a critical view of requirements and constantly provide feedback to lead engineer and team members regarding implementation possibilities
- diagnose startup problems in systemd/upstart
- debug with gdb when static code analysis is insufficient
- maintain CMake or Makefiles for large projects
- use predefined eBPF programs created Brendan Gregg for measuring behaviour in order to achieve application performance goals

experiences.title: Senior Linux Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Hired as C++ developer as part of the Network Diagnostics team on an embedded linux platform.
My responsibilities:
- develop C++ applications for a custom built linux system that conforms to automotive standards, which satisfy requirements to retrieve information and answer diagnostic protocol requests for network components in the target architecture as well as configure routes, multicast groups and check signal quality
- keep up to date with the hardware datasheets from manufacturers to configure and test network hw integrated in our project
- configure systemd components and read through C code in systemd to get information on its behavior regarding default settings and limitations on some network aspects
- udev kernel events screening and handling for network interfaces
- driver loading problem solving sometimes with reviewing code for externally delivered software
- device tree configuration checking and requesting features to BSP handling team
- refactoring or creating CMake build files for various projects on my team or others
- coaching colleagues on how to write easily maintainable S.O.L.I.D. code
- pushing for refactoring the pieces of our code that make it fragile or rigid by employing well known design patterns that allow unit testing with Google Mock and increase coverage
- keeping up to date with our client's internal architecrure and datasheets for relevant hardware involved in networking
- flashing the hardware with the latest firmware and solving problems that may rise from this as sometimes documentation is poor and pairing with colleagues yields good results and solves issues
- encouraging the team to get a more agile approach to solving tasks so that our results are visible and more time can be  saved and spent on developing our technical skills and low-level knowledge as well as the overall team C++ and object oriented design.
By far the most fulfilling job of my career.

experiences.title: Senior Linux Software Engineer (Harman division)
experiences.jobDescription: Hired as part of a project using C++. After 3 months the contract was automatically relayed to Harman and the transfer was seamless for me. The job responsibilities were the same as with Harman.

experiences.title: Software Engineer at ING Services
experiences.jobDescription:
Summit DevOps team

- respond to tickets for production issues on Summit system
- adapt scripts for internal nightly batch scheduling system
- assess impact of decomissioning Summit system

experiences.title: Software Developer
experiences.jobDescription:
Hired as C++ developer, the employer having interest in my Windows GUI development experience in order to work on it's MFC based application.
Worked on a legacy healthcare system application for patient record management.
Main technologies used:
 - Win32 API - Timers, threads, message hooks
 - Microsoft Foundation Classes - for the application GUI
 - C# and ActiveX for integrating with another host application

Worked on a legacy Document Managent Management application for 3 months where I learned a few new technologies and applied that knowledge to fix some issues and do some new developments.

Main technologies used:
 - Win32 API - Timers
 - ATL/COM - for integrating back end with C# front end through tlbimp generated assemblirs
 - MFC - old GUI
 - WCF and WPF for the new architecture built over the legacy core of the application

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Good opportunity to work and live abroad.
Main technology: C++ on Solaris 8/10 + some bash scripting and C# front end class adaptation to make the right calls to the back end.
Wrote Summit Back-End extensions, Straight Through Processing enhancements, Document Templates and FT Interfaces.
Dealt mostly with BOND and Repo trades and collaborated with MOA employees to establish clear and functionally correct specifications.

experiences.title: Software Developer
experiences.jobDescription: Wrote C++ applications for tachograph certificate validation, X.509 certificate validation on e-mail attachments on Dovecot and Exchange server setups and received a training for programming java cards on Muhlbauer card printers.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
C++ Software Engineer in the Back Office Team.
Created fixes and learned the architecture of the Summit system implemented by MISYS.
Trades dealt with: Swaps, Bonds, Repos, Deal Payments, Equity Swaps, FX Options, Fx Forward, Forward Rate Agreement, Equity Options, Total Return swaps and Credit default swaps. Each instrument was represented in the application as C++ classes and had a specific structure. Any extension, modification or bug fix was done in these classes as part of the daily job.
Wrote fixes and enhanced the functionality in Generic Straight through processing servers, such as the document server, dispatch server, flow generator, settlement server, netting and presettlement server. 
Wrote back-end objects for different views and implemented the corresponding functionality in the FT interface by using the Summit classes and Microsoft Infragistics.

experiences.title: Software Developer
experiences.jobDescription:
C++ and C# development.
Managing build trees.
MFC Development.
Documentation Writing.

recommendationsReceived.name: Alex Stefan
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Linux & Networking Systems | Infrastructure Automation | Python | CI/CD
recommendationsReceived.context: Alex worked with Razvan-Adrian on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working with Razvan, and I can confidently say that he’s an exceptional engineer with deep expertise in C++, Python, and Linux. His contributions to our projects have been significant, and I’ve been particularly impressed by his technical acumen and problem-solving capabilities.

    Razvan has delivered numerous presentations on Linux and C++, showcasing his deep understanding and expertise in these areas. His presentations are always thorough and insightful, providing valuable knowledge that has enhanced our team's skills. Razvan’s ability to break down complex concepts and explain them clearly makes his sessions both engaging and highly educational.

     I highly recommend Razvan to any company, as he is not only an exceptional engineer with deep expertise but also a fantastic colleague. His technical skills and problem-solving abilities make him a valuable asset to any team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Alin Ungureanu
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Linux C++ Engineer at Riverbed Technology
recommendationsReceived.description:
I’ve had the privilege of both working alongside Razvan and managing him directly at Riverbed, and I can confidently say that he is truly an exceptional professional. He has a knack for viewing challenges from different perspectives, which enables him to develop innovative solutions to complex problems. 

Razvan’s impact on the teams he has worked with has been tremendous. Not only does he possess deep expertise across multiple technological areas, but he also actively shares his knowledge with others. His technical presentations have added significant value to the company.

Razvan is familiar in cutting-edge software concepts and consistently promotes the adoption of best practices and emerging trends across the projects he has contributed to. In addition to his technical skills, Razvan is a natural innovator, always pushing boundaries to find more efficient and effective ways to solve problems.

I have no doubt that he will continue to excel in any future endeavor.

recommendationsReceived.name: Alexandra Drobut
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer | Snyk
recommendationsReceived.context: Razvan-Adrian was senior to Alexandra but didn't manage Alexandra directly
recommendationsReceived.description: I am grateful to have worked with Razvan in the complex universe of Riverbed products. Every time I was blocked by the complexity of a problem, Razvan guided me towards solutions and offered me answers. His intrinsic perseverance to learn things in detail and the ability to pass on his knowledge, motivates the people around him to grow and tackle complex technical challenges. Always up to date with the state-of-art features of different technology stacks, always curious. Thank you for the great colaboration. Wishing you luck and energy for everything you propose to achieve.

recommendations.name: Vlad Hornai
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer at Riverbed Technology
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian worked with Vlad but on different teams
recommendations.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working with Vlad, and I am truly impressed by his growth as an emerging engineer. He has tackled some of the most challenging tasks, showcasing remarkable tenacity and dedication. Vlad never shies away from hard work; instead, he embraces it with a positive attitude.

What stands out about Vlad is his eagerness to learn. He actively collaborates with more senior developers, seeking guidance and absorbing knowledge to enhance his skills. This determination has led him to successfully deliver on his projects, demonstrating his capability and commitment to excellence. He's also very knowledgeable in machine learning and I learned a lot about this area I've never had any interaction with after discussing with him.

I have no doubt that Vlad will continue to excel in his career, and I highly recommend him to any team looking for a driven and dedicated engineer. It’s been a privilege to watch him grow and contribute.

recommendations.name: Alex Stefan
recommendations.subtitle: Linux & Networking Systems | Infrastructure Automation | Python | CI/CD
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian worked with Alex but on different teams
recommendations.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working with Alex, and I can confidently say he’s an outstanding Python engineer with impressive Linux skills. His contributions to Riverbed projects has been significant, and I’ve been particularly impressed by his reporting capabilities and the clarity he brings to his work.

Our discussions on Linux have been enlightening, and I’ve learned a lot from his insights. Additionally, Alex delivered an excellent presentation on his projects, providing valuable perspectives on areas beyond my own focus.

His dedication and proficiency make him a great asset to our team, and I look forward to seeing his continued growth and impact. I highly recommend Alex to any organization looking for a talented and driven engineer.

recommendations.name: Dennis Hilbert
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer at Riverbed Technology
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian was senior to Dennis but didn't manage Dennis directly
recommendations.description:
I have had the pleasure of working with Dennis, and I can confidently say that he is an impressive engineer with a bright future ahead of him. Dennis has consistently demonstrated a strong ability to seek out requirements and tackle issues independently. He approaches each task with a clear understanding, ensuring that he doesn’t jump in without the necessary knowledge, which minimizes the risk of introducing bugs.

What stands out most about Dennis is his confidence and strong ethical standards. He projects a level of professionalism that is commendable for someone at his stage in their career. His proactive approach and commitment to delivering quality work make him a valuable asset to any team.

I highly recommend Dennis to anyone looking for a dedicated engineer who brings both skill and integrity to the table.

recommendations.name: Mihai D.
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer at Riverbed Technology
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian worked with Mihai on the same team
recommendations.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working closely with Mihai, and I can confidently say he’s one of the most thorough and skilled C++ programmers I’ve encountered. Mihai has an impressive ability to approach problems with a calm, thoughtful mindset. He dives deep into every issue, ensuring that his understanding is solid before delivering a solution.

What sets Mihai apart is not just his technical expertise, but also his perseverance. He’s tackled some of the most complex and tedious tasks, often involving a high volume of changes, with precision and dedication. His approach to problem-solving is meticulous, and he’s consistently demonstrated the ability to deliver high-quality results, no matter the challenge.

It’s been a privilege to collaborate with Mihai, and I highly recommend him to any team looking for a top-tier developer with a strong work ethic and a passion for excellence.

recommendations.name: Alin Ungureanu
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Linux C++ Engineer at Riverbed Technology
recommendations.description:
I’ve had the pleasure of working with Alin at Riverbed, both as a peer and reporting directly to him, and it’s been a great journey. Alin is incredibly agile, able to foresee potential issues and address them before they become problems. As a developer, there’s no task he can’t handle, and his thorough approach ensures nothing is left to chance.

As a manager, he’s fully committed to doing the job right. He takes deep responsibility for the projects he leads, always showing he’s a true team player, working alongside everyone to overcome obstacles and deliver results.

Above all, Alin is a great person to work with—someone who supports his team and consistently brings out the best in those around him. It’s been a privilege to learn from and collaborate with him.

recommendations.name: Alexandra Drobut
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Software Engineer | Snyk
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian worked with Alexandra on the same team
recommendations.description:
An engineering powerhouse that can solve any task no matter how difficult while also transfering her knowledge to the team. Each time Alexandra held a presentation we could see her academic ability to transfer her knowledge while at the same time leaving everybody impressed at the depth of each of her analyses. She's a great addition to any team and a great miss where she isn't.
Best of luck in your future endeavors!

recommendations.name: Andrada Nyirestyan
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer
recommendations.context: Razvan-Adrian worked with Andrada on the same team
recommendations.description:
I had the opportunity of working with Andrada on a large legacy project within Riverbed and the most impressing of her abilities was being able to summarise and share her knowledge with the rest of the team while at the same time handling her tasks and always communicating efficiently in every process she was part off.
She will make a great addition to any group in need for an excellent team-player

summary: Razvan-Adrian C. is currently at Microsoft and presents himself as a technically grounded software professional who combines hands-on cloud/configuration knowledge with career coaching, hiring insight, and public speaking. His posts regularly cover remote work and deep focus, developer productivity and evaluation pitfalls, recruiting and hiring practices, and practical career development advice for juniors and mid-level engineers. He balances personal milestones (certifications, a new book, conference talks) with thoughtful commentary on industry trends like bootcamps, portfolio careers, and the tradeoffs between gig work and team maintainability. His tone mixes candid personal reflection, thoughtful citations (quotes and article excerpts), and practical TL;DR takeaways—often aimed at junior-to-senior career growth and hiring managers. Content shows a pattern of community engagement: promoting peers, sharing job leads, offering free resources, and soliciting interactions to help those impacted by COVID or early in their careers. Overall, he differentiates himself by pairing concrete technical credentials (certs, talks) with a clear focus on people, hiring ethics, and pragmatic advice for career progression.
highlights: Thoughtful Critic of Developer Evaluation Practices
keywords: Software development as a team sport
